justifications.clarity: All six sections open with motivating context boxes or clear introductory paragraphs. Every mathematical symbol is introduced before use. Definitions are formally stated with named environments. The ReAct trajectory example with a concrete TSLA filing interaction trace, and the portfolio Q&A interaction example, are particularly clear. The three-tier memory taxonomy (Definition 4.4) provides a crisp, accessible framework for non-specialist readers.
justifications.rigor: The PRA loop definition (Def 4.2) explicitly declares all four spaces including the environment state space E. The RAG marginal (Eq. 4.4) is corrected to proper probabilistic form with explicit normalisation constraint. BM25 IDF is explicitly defined in-line with the BM25 equation. The hybrid search formulation addresses the scale mismatch between cosine similarity and BM25 by introducing normalised scores and the RRF alternative. The chained-hash audit trail (Def 4.11) is formally defined. The ReAct trajectory, Reflexion loop, and robust tool calling definitions are all internally consistent.
justifications.completeness: All six outline sections are fully developed with all 22 subsections containing substantive content (no placeholders remain). Two Python code listings illustrate tool registration and fact-grounded generation. Five RAGAS evaluation metrics are defined. Five finance applications (earnings analysis, report generation, portfolio assistants, filing search, trading signals) are developed in depth with worked examples. All four prompt injection defences are covered. The regulatory landscape (EU AI Act, MiFID II, SEC proposed rules) is addressed in the governance section. Bibliography file adds 18 new BibTeX entries covering all major topics.
justifications.pedagogy: Eight explicit learning objectives open the chapter. Context boxes open all six major sections with concrete real-world scenarios. Nine numbered examples span the full chapter, from PRA loop for earnings analysis to portfolio Q&A interaction. Six deepdive boxes address technically advanced material without interrupting the main narrative flow. The progression from single-agent architecture through multi-agent systems to RAG and deployed applications is logical and cumulative. The signal staleness remark adds a practitioner caution that prevents over-interpretation of text-based trading signal results.
justifications.style: Academic register is consistent throughout. LaTeX environments (definition, example, remark, deepdive, context) are used correctly and consistently. No paragraph headings or bold text used as structural elements. Citations use \citet{} and \cite{} consistently with authoryear style. The code listings use the lstlisting environment with appropriate language and caption parameters. One minor code quality note: the unused `Optional` import in Listing 4.1 could be removed, but this does not affect LaTeX compilation or pedagogical clarity.

details: GAP (resolved): def:pra-loop did not declare E (environment state space) — added to Let clause
NOTATION (resolved): RAG marginal summed over R(q,D) set rather than full corpus — corrected to sum over D with normalisation constraint noted
NOTATION (resolved): BM25 IDF term referenced but not defined — added explicit IDF formula with N and n(q_i)
ERROR (resolved): hybrid search linear combination mixed cosine \in [-1,1] and unbounded BM25 without normalisation — introduced min-max normalised scores and RRF alternative

editor_changes: Converted 3 passive constructions to active voice
Split 2 long sentences (>40 words) into shorter sentences
Removed 1 redundant phrase ('the tool specification that the model receives')
